back_translation: Six days before the Passover festival, Jesus came to the village of Bethany, where Lazarus lived, the one whom Jesus had caused to rise again from the dead.
They therefore prepared an evening meal for Him there. Martha was the one serving, and Lazarus was one of those sitting at the table together with Him.

Mary therefore took about half a liter of pure perfumed oil of very valuable nard, and anointed it on the feet of Jesus, and wiped His feet with her hair. The whole house was filled with the fragrance of that oil.
But Judas Iscariot, one of His disciples, the one who would later betray Him, said,
“Why did they not sell this perfumed oil for three hundred denarii, and then give the money to the poor?”
Judas said this not because he was concerned about the poor, but because he was a thief; he was the one who carried the money bag and used to embezzle the money put into it.
Jesus said, “Leave her alone. She has kept this perfumed oil for the day of preparing My body for burial.
You always have the poor with you, but you will not always have Me with you.”
A great crowd of Jewish people learned that Jesus was staying there, and they came not only because of Jesus, but also to see Lazarus, whom He had caused to rise again from the dead.
The chief priests therefore plotted to kill Lazarus also,
because, on account of Lazarus, many Jewish people had turned away from them, and were believing in Jesus.
The next day a great crowd that had come to the Passover festival heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem.
They therefore took palm branches and went out to meet Him, shouting, “Hosanna! ‘Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord!’ Blessed is the King of Israel as well!”
Jesus found a young donkey and sat on it, as it has been written,
‘Do not fear, daughter of Zion. Behold, your King is coming, sitting on a young donkey.’
At first His disciples did not understand these things, but when Jesus had received glory, they remembered that these things had been written about Him, and that they had done these things to Him.
The crowd that had been with Him when He called Lazarus out of the tomb and caused him to rise from the dead, testified about this.
Because of this, another crowd went out to meet Him, because they had heard that He had performed this sign.
The Pharisees therefore said among themselves, “Look, we are not succeeding at anything. See, the whole world has all gone after him.”
There were some Greeks among those who had gone up to worship at the festival.
They therefore came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and asked him, “Esteemed sir, we would like to see Jesus.”
Philip went to tell Andrew, then Andrew and Philip came and told Jesus.
Jesus answered them, “The hour has come for the Son of Man to receive glory.
Amen, amen, I say to you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the ground and dies first, it will remain only a single grain. But if it dies, it will bear much fruit.
Whoever loves his life will lose it. But whoever hates his life in this world will keep it for eternal life.
If anyone serves Me, let him follow Me. And where I am, My servant will also be there. If anyone serves Me, the Father will bestow honor on him.
Now My soul is troubled, and what shall I say? ‘Father, please save Me from this hour’? No, for it was for this very reason that I came to this hour.
Father, may Your name receive glory.” At that time a voice came from heaven saying, “I have made it receive glory, and I will make it receive glory again.”
The crowd that was standing there and heard it said it was thunder. Others said an angel had spoken to Him.
Jesus answered, “This voice did not come for My sake, but for yours.
Now is the time of judgment of this world. Now the ruler of this world will be cast out.
And as for Me, when I have been lifted up from the earth, I will draw everyone to Me.”
He said this to indicate by what kind of death He would die.
The crowd therefore said to Him, “We have heard from the Law that the Christ remains forever. So why do You say, ‘The Son of Man must be lifted up’? Who is this Son of Man?”

Jesus therefore said to them, “The light is with you only a little while longer. Walk while you still have the light, so that the darkness may not overcome you. The one who walks in the darkness does not know where he is going.
While you still have the light, believe in the light, so that you may be sons of light.” When Jesus had said these things, He went away and hid Himself from them.
Although Jesus had performed many signs in their presence, they still did not believe in Him,
so that the words spoken by Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled, ‘Lord, who has believed our message? And to whom has the hand of the Lord been revealed?’
Therefore they were unable to believe, because Isaiah had also said in another passage,
‘He has caused their eyes to be blind and their hearts to be hardened, so that they cannot see with their eyes and understand with their hearts and turn back, lest I should heal them.’
Isaiah said these things because he had seen His glory and spoke about Him.
Nevertheless, many of the Jewish leaders also believed in Him, but for fear of the Pharisees they did not dare to confess Him openly, lest they be expelled from the synagogue.
For they loved honor from men more than honor from God.
Jesus cried out with a loud voice, “The one who believes in Me does not believe in Me alone, but also in the One who sent Me.
And the one who sees Me also sees the One who sent Me.
I have come into the world as a light, so that everyone who believes in Me will not remain in darkness any longer.
If anyone hears My words and does not keep them, I do not judge him. For I did not come to judge the world, but to save the world.
The one who rejects Me and does not receive My words has one who will judge him; the words I have spoken — those will be the judge of him on the last day.
For I have not spoken on My own, but the Father who sent Me has commanded Me what to speak and how to say it.
And I know that His command is eternal life. Therefore whatever I speak, I speak according to what the Father has told Me.”
